authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 570 - Community Development Block Grants
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's CDBG regulations are directly authoritative for community development administration because they define eligible activities, national objectives, program administration, records, and compliance constraints for CDBG-funded work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: activity setup, national-objective support, beneficiary evidence, record maintenance, completion readiness, and compliance escalation
used_for: eligible activity and national objective blockers
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from treating a roster or generic project plan as enough to advance CDBG work without the required activity, national-objective, beneficiary, and source-record evidence.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 91 - Consolidated Submissions for Community Planning and Development Programs
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 91 governs consolidated plan, annual action plan, citizen participation, and performance reporting requirements that provide the planning and reporting records for CPD program execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: action-plan alignment, citizen-participation evidence, CAPER input preparation, and planning-record boundary recognition
used_for: consolidated plan and action plan artifacts
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Keeps the specialist on administrative evidence and reporting support instead of rewriting plans, deciding public-comment effects, or making policy judgments.

authoritative_sources.title: Integrated Disbursement and Information System (IDIS)
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development / HUD Exchange
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD Exchange is HUD's official technical-assistance platform, and IDIS is the operational HUD system used by CPD grantees for activity setup, funding, draws, accomplishments, and CAPER reporting support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: system-of-record verification, activity setup, draw support, accomplishment entry, and CAPER evidence packaging
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Defines IDIS as a governing system of record so the lane cannot close or report work from informal trackers alone.

authoritative_sources.title: Community Planning and Development Monitoring Handbook 6509.2
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's CPD monitoring handbook is official HUD operating guidance for monitoring preparation, findings, concerns, corrective action, evidence review, and documentation in CPD programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: monitoring preparation, evidence review, finding or concern handling, corrective-action tracking, and closeout escalation
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Converts monitoring-related work into evidence packaging and owner routing rather than allowing the lane to sign findings or close corrective actions without authority.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 58 - Environmental Review Procedures for Entities Assuming HUD Environmental Responsibilities
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Part 58 governs responsible-entity environmental review and release-of-funds posture for HUD-assisted projects, creating mandatory blockers for execution and completion when environmental clearance is unresolved.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: environmental review status verification, choice-limiting-action recognition, release-of-funds escalation, and project-start blocker handling
used_for: scope or site change exception handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the lane from recommending commitment of funds or start of work when environmental review status is missing or changed by project scope.

authoritative_sources.title: 2 CFR Part 200 - Uniform Administrative Requirements, Cost Principles, and Audit Requirements for Federal Awards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Uniform Guidance is the government-wide control framework for federal awards and directly governs internal controls, procurement, subrecipient monitoring, performance reporting, financial management, records, and closeout for HUD-funded community development activities.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: subrecipient package review, procurement evidence checks, performance reporting, financial and draw support boundary handling, and closeout evidence control
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Forces completion and reporting outputs to tie financial and performance claims to official award records rather than informal status notes.

authoritative_sources.title: 24 CFR Part 75 - Economic Opportunities for Low- and Very Low-Income Persons
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Section 3 rules are directly relevant to HUD housing and community development financial assistance because they define labor-hour, contracting, opportunity, reporting, and recordkeeping evidence for covered projects.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Section 3 labor-hour tracking, contractor evidence review, reporting exception handling, and closeout blocker recognition
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Prevents the specialist from certifying completion or reporting compliance when Section 3 evidence is incomplete or unreviewed.

authoritative_sources.title: Real Estate Acquisition and Relocation
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HUD's relocation and real estate guidance is authoritative for HUD-assisted acquisition, rehabilitation, demolition, and displacement workflows, and identifies HUD's delegated relocation authority for these programs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: relocation or acquisition flag detection, notice and occupant record packaging, adjacent-lane routing, and stop-condition handling
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Makes displacement and acquisition questions a specialized handoff rather than a generic project administration step.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards for Internal Control in the Federal Government
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GAO's Green Book is the recognized federal internal-control standard for control activities, segregation of duties, documentation, monitoring, and auditability, all of which are necessary for validated community development evidence handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: audit-trail design, segregation of duties, control evidence review, monitoring support, and completion record sufficiency
used_for: segregation of duties and approval boundaries
authoritative_sources.practical_use_in_pack: Constrain outputs so they document what was checked and who owns approval rather than blending administration with signoff authority.

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and CPD context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm jurisdiction, grantee or subrecipient, program year, funding source, activity identifier, project site, service area, requested execution action, and source-of-truth systems before work can be accepted.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Activity setup and prerequisite verification
workflow_stages.description: Verify action-plan tie, eligible activity basis, national objective support, beneficiary or service-area method, environmental review status, funding and budget setup, and owner map.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Execution and subrecipient package administration
workflow_stages.description: Track accepted work items, subrecipient deliverables, procurement file state, invoice and draw support, inspection records, Section 3 data, beneficiary evidence, and activity notes through explicit lifecycle states.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework routing
workflow_stages.description: Hold or return work when environmental status changes, project scope shifts, beneficiary evidence is incomplete, procurement files are missing, Section 3 records are incomplete, relocation flags appear, or source records conflict.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Reporting, monitoring, and CAPER evidence packaging
workflow_stages.description: Assemble IDIS accomplishment support, performance and financial report inputs, monitoring evidence, corrective-action trackers, and CAPER packages without making compliance findings or approving official submissions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Completion readiness and closeout evidence capture
workflow_stages.description: Confirm required inspection, beneficiary, expenditure, subrecipient, IDIS, monitoring, environmental, Section 3, relocation, and closeout artifacts before issuing a completion or fulfillment record.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: consolidated plan, annual action plan, citizen participation record, substantial amendment record, and CAPER calendar
CDBG or CPD activity setup records, IDIS activity IDs, funding source, budget setup, draw and voucher support, and accomplishment records
eligible activity basis, national objective support, LMI or beneficiary evidence, service-area maps, roster or portfolio trackers, and demographic or income support
subrecipient agreements, work plans, invoices, procurement files, contract records, contractor and labor-hour records, Section 3 reports, and monitoring files
environmental review records, release-of-funds posture, floodplain or historic preservation flags, scope and site-change notes, and responsible-entity approvals
relocation or acquisition notices, occupant records, URA or Section 104(d) flags, real property acquisition files, and legal or relocation-owner notes
inspection reports, site photos, closeout checklists, corrective-action logs, monitoring letters, finance records, audit logs, and tenant policy extracts
systems_of_record: HUD IDIS or tenant IDIS-derived reporting system
grants management or CPD program administration platform
finance, voucher, draw, and general ledger systems
environmental review record system or responsible entity repository
GIS, service-area, beneficiary, income, or case management system
inspection, construction management, or site closeout system
Section 3 labor-hour and contractor reporting records
tenant policy, authority, and approval registry

allowed: Classify community development execution work as in scope, blocked, handoff-ready, or unresolved based on documented CPD records and tenant authority maps.
Advance, hold, rework, or complete administrative lifecycle states when source records, evidence thresholds, and owner state support the transition.

Prepare execution status updates, exception summaries, completion evidence records, program or portfolio summaries, monitoring evidence packets, CAPER support packages, and escalation notes without claiming final approval authority.
Request minimum tenant facts, systems-of-record access, and authoritative records needed to continue safely.
escalate: National objective, eligible activity, environmental review, beneficiary method, procurement file, Section 3, relocation, draw support, monitoring, or closeout evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
A project scope, site, budget, schedule, or beneficiary method changes in a way that may affect environmental, eligibility, procurement, relocation, or reporting posture.
Public source, tenant policy, grant agreement, monitoring instruction, or system-of-record data materially conflict on whether work may advance or close.
The request requires fair housing, civil-rights, legal, environmental, relocation, procurement, engineering, inspection, finance, draw, policy, or executive approval authority.
refuse: Mark an activity, subrecipient task, reporting package, monitoring item, or project complete without required traceable completion evidence.
Commit HUD-assisted funds, approve draws, certify environmental clearance, decide relocation entitlement, approve procurement action, or certify compliance without delegated authority.
Backdate, delete, conceal, or manipulate CPD, IDIS, beneficiary, inspection, monitoring, finance, or closeout records to satisfy a stakeholder preference.
Treat community development as generic program tracking when HUD, tenant, and grant-specific records materially govern the answer.

domain_failure_modes: the lane advances a CDBG or CPD activity without confirming IDIS activity identity, funding source, action-plan tie, eligible activity, and national-objective support
beneficiary, LMI, service-area, or accomplishment evidence is collapsed into informal status notes
environmental review, release-of-funds, scope change, or choice-limiting-action issues are treated as routine scheduling details
subrecipient reimbursement or closeout is packaged without procurement, invoice, inspection, Section 3, draw, or monitoring evidence
relocation or acquisition flags are ignored and the lane attempts to decide URA notice or payment sufficiency
local policy and HUD or federal award controls conflict but the lane chooses one without owner resolution
the lane treats CAPER, monitoring, or IDIS reporting as a compliance certification rather than evidence-backed administrative support
completion state is made irreversible even though later evidence invalidates beneficiary, environmental, inspection, or closeout support

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction and grantee-specific community development procedures
unresolved_ambiguities.description: State administering agencies, entitlement grantees, local citizen participation plans, and grant agreements can impose stricter procedures than the public baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant jurisdiction, program year, action plan, policy, grant agreement, system-of-record, and authority matrix before execution state changes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Final eligibility, environmental, relocation, procurement, and draw approval authority
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources establish the need for these controls but do not delegate final decision authority to this administrative execution lane.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Treat these as refusal or adjacent-lane routing triggers and limit this specialist to evidence packaging, status, and owner tracking.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Source conflicts between local manuals and federal baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.description: Tenant manuals may be outdated or less strict than HUD, eCFR, grant agreement, monitoring, or federal award controls.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Escalate conflicts to the tenant-designated policy, grant compliance, legal, or HUD liaison owner and preserve all source citations.
